questions.question: Which HTML tag is used to link an external CSS stylesheet?
questions.explanation: The <link> tag with rel="stylesheet" and an href attribute is used to link an external CSS file to an HTML document.

questions.question: What is the correct CSS syntax for making all paragraph text red?
questions.explanation: CSS rules follow the pattern: selector { property: value; }. The element selector 'p' targets all paragraphs, and 'color' is the property for text color.

questions.question: Which method of applying CSS has the highest specificity?
questions.explanation: Inline styles applied via the style attribute directly on an element have the highest specificity, overriding both internal and external stylesheets.

questions.question: Where should the <style> tag be placed in an HTML document?
questions.explanation: The <style> tag is placed inside the <head> section of an HTML document to define internal CSS rules.

questions.question: The HTML attribute used to apply inline CSS directly to an element is the ___ attribute.
questions.explanation:
The style attribute is added directly to an HTML element to apply inline CSS, e.g.,

.

questions.question: In the <link> tag, the ___ attribute specifies the relationship between the HTML document and the linked file.
questions.explanation: The rel attribute in <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css"> specifies the relationship as a stylesheet.
